channel.title: NYT > Books > Book Review
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Book Review: ‘Reproductive Wrongs,’ by Sarah Ruden
item.description: In “Reproductive Wrongs,” the classicist Sarah Ruden traces efforts to exert political control over family planning back 2,000 years.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Szalai
item.category: Dobbs v Jackson Women's Health Organization (Supreme Court Decision)
item.media:description: The Roman poet Ovid’s anti-abortion poems were part of an effort to ingratiate himself with the emperor, Sarah Ruden argues.

item.title: Book Review: ‘Field Notes From an Extinction,’ by Eoghan Walls
item.description: “Field Notes From an Extinction,” by Eoghan Walls, follows a naturalist who wants to study birds but ends up with a much harder task.
item.dc:creator: Graeme Macrae Burnet

item.title: Book Review: ‘Plastic Inc.,’ by Beth Gardiner
item.description: A new book by the journalist Beth Gardiner argues that oil companies are upping production of the material as a safeguard against falling revenue.
item.dc:creator: Zoë Schlanger
item.category: Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet (Book)
item.media:credit: Tonje Thilesen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Plastic took over the globe through decades of intensive marketing, political maneuvering and flat-out deceit, Beth Gardiner maintains in “Plastic Inc.”

item.title: Book Review: ‘A Far-Flung Life,’ by M.L. Stedman
item.description: In M.L. Stedman’s new novel, “A Far-Flung Life,” the beauty and breadth of her setting stand in counterpoint to the horrors of the human lives playing out upon it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Preston

item.title: The 10 Best Books of 2025
item.description: The staff of The New York Times Book Review choose the year’s top fiction and nonfiction.
item.category: The Loneliness of Sonia and Sunny (Book)
There Is No Place for Us: Working and Homeless in America (Book)
Mother Emanuel: Two Centuries of Race, Resistance, and Forgiveness in One Charleston Church (Book)
A Marriage at Sea: A True Story of Love, Obsession, and Shipwreck (Book)
Wild Thing: A Life of Paul Gauguin (Book)

item.title: Sarah J. Maas Announces Next Books in ‘A Court of Thorns and Roses’ Series
item.description: The sixth book is scheduled to be released on Oct. 27, 2026, and the seventh on Jan. 12, 2027, the author announced on the “Call Her Daddy” podcast.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.category: A Court of Thorns and Roses (Book)
item.media:description: Sarah J. Maas did not share the titles or cover art for her new books, but acknowledged that the new installments had been “a long time coming.”

item.title: Book Review: ‘Lake Effect,’ by Cynthia D’Aprix Sweeney
item.description: Cynthia D’Aprix Sweeney’s new novel, “Lake Effect,” is the latest in a specific contemporary subgenre: “Four Adult Siblings Reconvene to Rehash Their Privileged but Fraught Adolescence.”
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Makkai

item.title: Book Review: ‘Every Time We Say Goodbye,’ by Ivana Sajko
item.description: Ivana Sajko’s novel “Every Time We Say Goodbye” explores personal and political crises in lengthy, lyrical sentences.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kalfus

item.title: Why Bethany Collins Transcribed ‘Moby-Dick’ by Hand
item.description: For Bethany Collins, Herman Melville’s novel is rife with centuries-old political anxieties that still resonate today.
item.dc:creator: Elly Fishman
item.category: Museum of Contemporary Art Denver (MCA Denver)

item.title: Hamnet, Hamlet and Oscar Wao: Three Lost Boys Across Time
item.description: In the stage versions of two beloved books, the most impressive moments emerge when the productions stray from the source material.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.category: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao (Play)
The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao (Book)

item.title: Book Review: ‘Muv,’ by Rachel Trethewey
item.description: In “Muv,” the biographer Rachel Trethewey looks at the Mitford family matriarch.
item.dc:creator: Meryl Gordon
item.category: Muv: The Story of the Mitford Girls' Mother (Book)

item.title: Book Review: ‘Repetition,’ by Vigdis Hjorth
item.description: In Vigdis Hjorth’s novel “Repetition,” a writer recalls a pivotal period of transformation, sex and family crises.
item.dc:creator: S.C. Cornell

item.title: Book Review: ‘The Valley of Vengeful Ghosts,’ by Kim Fu
item.description: In “The Valley of Vengeful Ghosts,” a therapist’s home turns into a nightmare manifestation of her sadness and grief.
item.dc:creator: Kiersten White

item.title: Book Review: ‘Now I Surrender,’ by Álvaro Enrigue
item.description: Álvaro Enrigue’s new novel, “Now I Surrender,” weaves past and present in a baroque anti-Western set in contested borderlands.
item.dc:creator: Dwight Garner

item.title: Book Review: ‘Backstitch,’ by Marian Mitchell Donahue
item.description: “Backstitch,” a novel by Marian Mitchell Donahue, examines the stark contrast between public talent and private troubles.
item.dc:creator: Cara Blue Adams

item.title: Book Review: ‘El Paso,’ by Jazmine Ulloa
item.description: In “El Paso,” Jazmine Ulloa paints her hometown as a microcosm for all that is good and bad about the United States.
item.dc:creator: Paul Begala
item.category: El Paso: Five Families and One Hundred Years of Blood, Migration, Race, and Memory (Book)
item.media:description: Pancho Villa in El Paso in the 1910s.

item.title: The Art of Murder
item.description: Our columnist on the month’s best new mysteries.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Weinman

item.title: The American Comedian Who Became a Funnyman in China
item.description: Jesse Appell left everything behind to pursue a comedy career in China, where Western-style club comedy was just finding its footing.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.category: This Was Funnier in China: An American Comedian's Cross-Cultural Journey (Book)
item.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jesse Appell turned his fascination with Chinese culture into a decade-long comedy career there. He tells the story in a new memoir, “This Was Funnier in China: An American Comedian’s Cross-Cultural Journey.”

item.title: Christina Applegate’s New Memoir Is Furious, Funny and Profane
item.description: Funny, furious and profane, “You With the Sad Eyes” finds the TV star facing childhood trauma and reflecting on the limits imposed by illness.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Egan
item.media:description: Though she has retired from acting, Christina Applegate (here in front of her home) appeared onstage at the Emmy Awards in 2024.

item.title: Book Review: ‘The Disappearing Act,’ by Maria Stepanova
item.description: In Maria Stepanova’s novel “The Disappearing Act,” an accidental stopover in a foreign town leads to personal change.
item.dc:creator: John Self

item.title: Book Review: ‘The Violet Hour,’ by James Cahill
item.description: James Cahill’s “The Violet Hour” contrasts the artifice of blue-chip modern art with the messy personal lives of the people who create and consume it.
item.dc:creator: Jay McInerney

item.title: Thrilling Slasher Books for ‘Scream’ and Horror Fans
item.description: These 13 bloodthirsty tales will keep you up at night with clever thrills and heart-pounding action.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Graham Jones

item.title: Book Club: Let’s Talk About ‘Wuthering Heights’
item.description: Emily Brontë’s classic Gothic romance is the basis for a new movie. It’s also more bonkers than you remember.
item.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; Inset cover: via Penguin Books

item.title: Book Club: Read ‘Kin,’ by Tayari Jones, With the Book Review
item.description: In March, the Book Review Book Club will read and discuss Tayari Jones’s new novel, about two motherless girls and their lifelong search for family.
item.dc:creator: MJ Franklin

item.title: Book Review: ‘A World Appears,’ by Michael Pollan
item.description: “A World Appears” explores what makes you you.
item.dc:creator: David Eagleman
item.category: A World Appears: A Journey Into Consciousness (Book)

item.title: 27 New Books to Read in March: Tana French, Liza Minnelli, Cat Sebastian and More
item.description: Novels by Tana French, Yann Martel and Cat Sebastian; memoirs by Christina Applegate and Liza Minnelli; a Judy Blume biography and more.

item.title: Picture Books and Graphic Novels for Mo Willems Fans
item.description: Twelve recommendations for young fans of Mo Willems.
item.dc:creator: Travis Jonker
item.category: Dont Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus! (Book)
Lone Wolf Goes to the Library (Book)

item.title: After 50 Years in the Shadows, a Tenacious First-Time Novelist Steps Out Front
item.description: For 50 years, Patricia Finn kept to the background and told other people’s stories. Now, in “The Golden Boy,” she’s finally telling one of her own.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Egan
item.media:credit: Alana Paterson for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Life does have a way of rearranging your plans,” says Patricia Finn, in her Vancouver home,

item.title: 7 New Books Our Editors Love This Week
item.description: Reading recommendations from critics and editors at The New York Times.
item.category: Young Man in a Hurry: A Memoir of Discovery (Book)

item.title: Teresa de Lauretis, Coiner (and Critic) of Queer Theory, Dies at 87
item.description: She came up with the term as the title of a 1990 conference but saw its later popularity as a little superficial.
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub
item.media:description: Teresa de Lauretis in 2015. The debut of “queer theory” as a term came in the title of an academic conference she organized and in an associated issue of a feminist journal.

item.title: Rose Lesniak, Poet Who Rescued Children and Trained Dogs, Dies at 70
item.description: A magnetic personality, she reinvented herself twice, bringing the same spirit to investigating child abuse and communing with dogs that she did to writing poetry.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald

item.title: Melissa Auf der Maur, a ’90s Rock Linchpin, Is Spilling Her Stories
item.description: The bassist and photographer who logged time in Hole and Smashing Pumpkins unpacks one of the most creative and chaotic times of her life in a new memoir.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik and Lucia Bell-Epstein
item.category: Even the Good Girls Will Cry: A '90s Rock Memoir (Book)
item.media:credit: Lucia Bell-Epstein for The New York Times

item.title: Book Review: ‘Fashioning the Crown,’ by Justine Picardie
item.description: In a new book, the biographer Justine Picardie romps through a century of royal wardrobes.
item.dc:creator: Eva Wolchover
item.category: Elizabeth II, Queen of Great Britain
item.media:description: Although Wallis Simpson’s marital status may have been the official objection, she and Edward VIII had also scandalized courtiers with their jaunty sportswear.
